findings.finding: The plan's central premise 'After S4 the core no longer imports these modules' is false: S4 (ticket 6673-7636-a116-4f90) still has status 'open', and the codebase shows active direct imports of acli/adf/outbound_fields in run_differs.py, apply_inbound.py, outbound_differ.py, fetcher.py, _attestation.py, and adapters/jira/jira_fields.py. The plan's 'closed, enumerable set' claim and safety rationale rest on a precondition that has not been met.
findings.location: Context / Problem section: 'After S4 [rebar:6673-7636-a116-4f90] the core no longer imports these modules'
scenarios: The plan's 'atomic' change scope is understated: moving the eight modules will break all of these active import sites, not just the enumerated 'importer repointing' sites.
If S4 is truly a prerequisite, executing this story before S4 completes will require significantly more edit surface than the plan describes.
findings.suggested_fix: Run `grep -r 'from rebar_reconciler import acli\|from rebar_reconciler.acli\|from rebar_reconciler.outbound_fields\|from rebar_reconciler.adf\|from rebar_reconciler.comment_limits\|from rebar_reconciler import acli_' src/` before proceeding to get the true importer list; add every hit to the edit-set.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate and update all hardcoded filesystem path constants for the moved modules in test files (ACLI_PATH, _ADF_PATH, _CL_PATH, tests/_engine_path.acli_path()), and broaden the structural grep gate to include path-literal patterns like `"rebar_reconciler" / "acli.py"` and `"rebar_reconciler" / "adf.py"`.
evidence: grep of `patch("rebar_reconciler.acli` returns zero hits across all test files
11+ test files contain `ACLI_PATH = SCRIPTS_DIR / "rebar_reconciler" / "acli.py"` hardcoded path constants
7+ test files contain `_ADF_PATH = SCRIPTS_DIR / "rebar_reconciler" / "adf.py"` and `_CL_PATH = SCRIPTS_DIR / "rebar_reconciler" / "comment_limits.py"`
tests/_engine_path.py:25-26 defines `acli_path() = engine_dir() / 'rebar_reconciler' / 'acli.py'`
Actual patch mechanism: `patch.object(applier_mod, "_load_acli", return_value=fake_acli_mod)` — object-level, not string-path-based
findings.finding: The plan describes the ~29 test patch surface as 'mock.patch("rebar_reconciler.acli*")' string targets. The actual test infrastructure uses `patch.object(applier_mod, "_load_acli", ...)` (object-level patching, immune to path changes) and hardcoded filesystem path constants (`ACLI_PATH = SCRIPTS_DIR / "rebar_reconciler" / "acli.py"`, `_ADF_PATH`, `_CL_PATH`) that will break after the move. The plan's edit-set and structural grep gate do not cover these path-literal sites.
findings.location: Context / Problem section: '~29-test patch surface — every mock.patch("rebar_reconciler.acli…") … string must be rewritten'; Testing section: 'Structural gate: grep finds zero references to the old root paths'
scenarios: After the move, tests loading acli.py via `ACLI_PATH = SCRIPTS_DIR / 'rebar_reconciler' / 'acli.py'` will get FileNotFoundError since acli.py no longer lives there.

The structural grep gate checking 'zero references to old root paths' will miss `patch.object` calls (correct) but will also miss the hardcoded `SCRIPTS_DIR / "rebar_reconciler" / "acli.py"` path constants unless the grep pattern explicitly targets them.
findings.suggested_fix: Run `grep -r '"rebar_reconciler" / "acli\|"rebar_reconciler" / "adf\|"rebar_reconciler" / "comment_limits\|"rebar_reconciler" / "outbound_fields' tests/` to find all path-literal sites; add them to the edit-set.

Plan says: 'Rewrite the ~29 test patch strings' — but says nothing about hardcoded spec_from_file_location paths
findings.finding: At least 11 test files contain hardcoded `spec_from_file_location` paths to acli.py, adf.py, and comment_limits.py at the OLD root location (e.g. `SCRIPTS_DIR / "rebar_reconciler" / "acli.py"`). These are distinct from mock.patch strings and will break on file move, but are not mentioned in the plan's edit-set.
findings.location: Testing section / tests/ — multiple test files
scenarios: After the move, any test that constructs a Path to the old root location of acli.py/adf.py/comment_limits.py/outbound_fields.py will get a non-existent path. spec_from_file_location will return None or raise, causing fixture setup to fail with confusing errors.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add all `from rebar_reconciler import acli*` and `import rebar_reconciler.acli*` sites in tests/ to the edit-set, updating them to the new `adapters.jira` paths (or to path-based spec_from_file_location calls), since these break on move and no shim is permitted.

test_comment_pagination.py:27: from rebar_reconciler import acli_cli_ops, acli_subprocess

Plan scope: 'no re-export shim'; plan patch-string section does not list these direct imports
findings.finding: Several test files do direct package-attribute imports (`from rebar_reconciler import acli`, `from rebar_reconciler import acli_rest`, `from rebar_reconciler import acli_cli_ops, acli_subprocess`, `import rebar_reconciler.acli as acli`) that will fail after the physical move with no shim permitted — but these consumers are not listed in the plan's edit-set.
findings.location: Edit-set / Scope sections — tests/unit and tests/external
scenarios: Once acli.py, acli_rest.py, acli_subprocess.py, acli_cli_ops.py are removed from the package root, `from rebar_reconciler import acli` resolves against the package's __init__.py / sys.path. Since these are loaded by path (not installed), the submodule won't be found under the new adapters.jira location via a bare `from rebar_reconciler import` statement. Tests fail with ImportError or AttributeError.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Revise the plan's patch-surface description to accurately reflect that tests use `patch.object` (path-agnostic, no rewrite needed) rather than string-based `mock.patch("rebar_reconciler.acli…")` calls — and redirect the edit-set focus to the actual breaking consumers.
evidence: Grep for 'mock.patch("rebar_reconciler.acli' returns zero hits
Grep for 'patch("rebar_reconciler.acli' returns zero hits
Observed pattern: patch.object(applier_mod, "_load_acli", return_value=fake_acli_mod) — dozens of occurrences
Plan: 'every mock.patch("rebar_reconciler.acli…") / patch.object string must be rewritten'
findings.finding: The plan characterises the test patch surface as '~29 mock.patch("rebar_reconciler.acli…")' string patches requiring rewrite, but the actual test code overwhelmingly uses `patch.object(module, "_load_acli", ...)` — a live-object patch that is path-agnostic. No string-based `mock.patch("rebar_reconciler.acli…")` calls were found in the codebase. The real migration burden is in hardcoded Path literals and direct `from rebar_reconciler import` statements (separate findings above), not string patch rewrites.
scenarios: Implementer spends effort searching for string-based patch targets that don't exist; meanwhile the actual breaking consumers (Path literals, direct imports) are missed or deprioritized.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the '~29 mock.patch string rewrite' claim with an accurate description: 'tests use patch.object via live module references (no rewrite needed); the breaking consumer set consists of hardcoded Path literals and direct `from rebar_reconciler import` statements enumerated in the edit-set.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `inbound_translate.py:323` (`lazy_load(_ADF_KEY_APPLIER, "adf.py")`) to the edit-set's named callsite update list alongside `inbound_fields.py:36` and `outbound_comments.py:84,100`.
evidence: Plan names only three non-moved callsites: `inbound_fields.py:36`, `outbound_comments.py:84,100` — `inbound_translate.py` is not listed.
Code at inbound_translate.py:323: `_AdfModule_Applier = lazy_load(_ADF_KEY_APPLIER, "adf.py")` — confirmed by search_files.
Plan states: 'Repoint the non-moved root modules that reference the moved files by sibling filename' — but misses inbound_translate.py as a fourth such module.
findings.finding: `inbound_translate.py:323` calls `lazy_load(_ADF_KEY_APPLIER, "adf.py")` — a non-moved root module loading adf by the old filename — but it is absent from the plan's named callsite update list. After the move, `_PACKAGE_DIR / "adf.py"` no longer exists, so any code path that triggers `_load_adf_module()` in `inbound_translate.py` will raise `ImportError` at runtime.
findings.location: Edit-set section / Approach section — named callsite list; also src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_translate.py:323
scenarios: Any reconcile pass that exercises inbound translation (e.g., processing an inbound Jira comment with ADF body) calls `_load_adf_module()` in inbound_translate.py, which calls `lazy_load(key, "adf.py")` against the old root path — FileNotFoundError at runtime.
Suite may not catch this if no test exercises the specific inbound_translate ADF path after the move, causing a production-only regression.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Edit-set section, extend the bullet 'Repoint the non-moved root modules...' to include: `inbound_translate.py:323` (`lazy_load(_ADF_KEY_APPLIER, "adf.py")` → `lazy_load(_ADF_KEY_APPLIER, "adapters/jira/adf.py")`).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand the test-side edit surface description in the Approach/Testing sections to explicitly include the hardcoded `Path`-literal references to moved files in test boilerplate (e.g. `ACLI_PATH`, `_ADF_PATH`, `_CL_PATH`, `ADF_PATH`, `acli_path()` in `_engine_path.py`, `RECONCILER_DIR / "adf.py"`, and the `outbound_fields.py` path in `test_commits_on_ticket.py`), and ensure AC3's grep pattern covers `Path` string literals in addition to `mock.patch` targets.
evidence: tests/_engine_path.py:26: `return engine_dir() / "rebar_reconciler" / "acli.py"` — a shared helper function, not a patch string.
tests/scripts/conftest.py:37: `ACLI_PATH = REPO_ROOT / ... / "rebar_reconciler" / "acli.py"` — module-level Path constant.
tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/apply/test_transition_error_soft_fail.py:44: `_ADF_PATH = SCRIPTS_DIR / "rebar_reconciler" / "adf.py"` — one of ~7 such files with identical patterns.
tests/interfaces/store/test_commits_on_ticket.py:119: direct path `/ "rebar_reconciler" / "outbound_fields.py"` used to read source text.
tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_reconcile_roundtrip.py:59: `RECONCILER_DIR / "adf.py"`.
tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_config.py:193: `_load_attr("outbound_fields.py", ...)` resolves relative to hardcoded engine/rebar_reconciler dir.
Plan says: 'Rewrite the ~29 test patch strings... atomically in the same change' — Path-based references are not patch strings and are not separately called out.
findings.finding: The plan scopes the test-side edit surface exclusively to '~29 test patch strings' (mock.patch/patch.object rewrites), but the actual test surface contains a second, distinct category of hardcoded `Path` references — module-level constants like `ACLI_PATH = SCRIPTS_DIR / "rebar_reconciler" / "acli.py"`, `_ADF_PATH = SCRIPTS_DIR / "rebar_reconciler" / "adf.py"`, `_CL_PATH = SCRIPTS_DIR / "rebar_reconciler" / "comment_limits.py"`, `ADF_PATH` in test_adf.py, `RECONCILER_DIR / "adf.py"` in the integration test, `/ "rebar_reconciler" / "outbound_fields.py"` in test_commits_on_ticket.py, and `tests/_engine_path.py:acli_path()` — that call `spec_from_file_location` with the old file path. These will fail with `FileNotFoundError` after the move. The plan's structural grep gate (AC3) could catch these but only if the grep is explicitly run against `Path(...)` string literals, not just `mock.patch` strings.
findings.location: Testing section / Acceptance Criteria AC3 / Scope section ('~29 test patch strings')
scenarios: After the move, `spec_from_file_location("rebar_reconciler.acli", _ADF_PATH)` in multiple test module preambles raises FileNotFoundError at collection time, preventing those test modules from loading at all.
tests/_engine_path.py:acli_path() is a shared helper — any test calling it will fail at collection time unless updated.

The plan's 'Teeth' test (temporarily leave one patch string at old path) catches mock.patch binding failures but does NOT validate that the Path-literal test preambles still resolve — a different failure mode.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State a concrete, anchored grep pattern in AC3 (and in the Structural gate) that covers all moved module names, e.g. `grep -rE 'rebar_reconciler\.(acli|adf|outbound_fields|comment_limits)' src/ tests/` returning zero hits.
evidence: AC3: 'grep for the old rebar_reconciler.acli* / root-adf/outbound_fields/comment_limits patch targets returns zero hits — verified by grep'
No concrete grep command or anchored pattern is stated; the three non-acli module names are bundled in prose without a pattern
VERIFY-COMMAND DEFECT TAXONOMY item (1): substring false-positive risk from an underspecified pattern
findings.finding: AC3's grep verification is not anchored to a concrete pattern covering all moved modules. The plan mentions grepping for 'old rebar_reconciler.acli* / root-adf/outbound_fields/comment_limits patch targets' but gives no actual grep command or word-boundary-anchored pattern. An executor could write a grep that only catches `acli` and silently miss stale `rebar_reconciler.adf`, `rebar_reconciler.outbound_fields`, and `rebar_reconciler.comment_limits` patch strings.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3; Testing — Structural gate
scenarios: Executor writes `grep -r 'rebar_reconciler.acli' tests/` and declares AC3 green, never checking for stale `rebar_reconciler.adf` / `rebar_reconciler.outbound_fields` / `rebar_reconciler.comment_limits` patch strings, leaving broken tests in the suite.
findings.suggested_fix:
Replace the prose description in AC3 with a single, copy-pasteable command:
```
grep -rE 'rebar_reconciler\.(acli[^.]*|adf|outbound_fields|comment_limits)' src/ tests/
```
and assert exit code 1 (no matches). Mirror this in the Structural gate section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend AC 1/2) that explicitly verifies `inbound_fields.py` and `outbound_comments.py` reference the moved modules at their new `adapters/jira/` subpath — e.g., via `grep` confirming zero old-path references in those two files, or a targeted loader/import test for those code paths.
evidence: 'inbound_fields.py:36 (adf_path = Path(__file__).parent / "adf.py") … must be updated to the adapters/jira/… subpath or they break at import'
'outbound_comments.py:84,100 (lazy_load(_ADF_KEY, "adf.py"), lazy_load(_COMMENT_LIMITS_KEY, "comment_limits.py")) must be updated'
No AC line reads: 'inbound_fields.py and outbound_comments.py reference the moved modules at their new subpath — verified by grep or targeted test.'
findings.finding: The Edit-set describes two non-moved files (`inbound_fields.py:36` and `outbound_comments.py:84,100`) that must be repointed to the new `adapters/jira/` subpath, but no Acceptance Criterion explicitly verifies that these two files were correctly updated. AC 2 covers only the loader, and AC 5 (suite green) is an indirect check that could mask a latent issue if those code paths aren't exercised by the full suite.
findings.location: Edit-set ('Repoint the non-moved root modules…'); AC list (no criterion for inbound_fields/outbound_comments repoint)
scenarios: The implementer moves the eight files and updates the loader, passes the structural grep gate (which checks the moved modules' old paths), but forgets to update `inbound_fields.py:36` — that hard-coded `Path(__file__).parent / 'adf.py'` silently resolves to a missing file at runtime, not caught until that code path executes.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC 2 or add AC 6: 'All hard-coded `Path(__file__).parent / "*.py"` and `lazy_load` calls in `inbound_fields.py` and `outbound_comments.py` reference the `adapters/jira/` subpath; `grep -n "adf.py\|comment_limits.py" src/…/inbound_fields.py src/…/outbound_comments.py` returns zero hits at the old bare filename — verified by grep.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the post-move grep gate (and pre-commit check) to cover `docs/`, ADRs, `AGENTS.md`, and any READMEs for references to the eight old root-level module paths, and update any hits found.
evidence: "grep finds zero references to the old root paths of the moved modules in src/ and tests/"
ABSENCE: no mention of scanning docs/, ADRs, or AGENTS.md for stale module-path references
findings.finding: The plan's structural grep gate covers `src/` and `tests/` but not documentation files (ADRs, AGENTS.md, READMEs, or inline doc references). If any of those reference the old root-level module paths (`rebar_reconciler/acli.py`, `rebar_reconciler/adf.py`, etc.), those references become stale and strand readers after the move.
findings.location: Testing § Structural gate / Acceptance Criteria § grep zero-references
scenarios: A developer reads ADR 0035 or AGENTS.md after the move and finds a reference to `rebar_reconciler/acli.py` at the package root, which no longer exists, causing confusion about canonical module locations.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a grep step: `grep -r 'rebar_reconciler/acli\|rebar_reconciler/adf\|rebar_reconciler/outbound_fields\|rebar_reconciler/comment_limits' docs/ *.md` and resolve any hits before closing the story.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document the `lazy_load(key, relative_subpath)` convention introduced by this story — at minimum in a docstring on `_loader.lazy_load` and/or in AGENTS.md — so future module relocations into sub-packages follow the same pattern without rediscovery.
evidence: "pass lazy_load(_ADF_KEY, 'adapters/jira/adf.py') — _PACKAGE_DIR / 'adapters/jira/adf.py' resolves correctly and leaves every other root lookup untouched"
ABSENCE: no mention of updating AGENTS.md, a loader docstring, or any design note to capture the new relative-subpath calling convention
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new internal loader convention — passing a relative subpath string (e.g., `"adapters/jira/adf.py"`) to `lazy_load` instead of a bare filename — but does not call for documenting this new contract in AGENTS.md or a code comment at the call-site, leaving the next developer who needs to move a module without guidance on how to invoke the loader correctly.
findings.location: Context / Problem § Loader update approach; Scope
scenarios: A future developer needs to relocate another root module to a sub-adapter and re-discovers the loader by trial-and-error, or incorrectly assumes bare filenames are the only supported form and writes a shim instead.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-line docstring to `lazy_load`: `'filename may be a bare name (root module) or a relative subpath (e.g. adapters/jira/adf.py) for sub-package modules.'` and add a bullet to AGENTS.md under the loader section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan (e.g., 'revert the single atomic commit and re-run suite to confirm recovery') to the Approach or a dedicated Rollback section, satisfying T4's requirement for a reversibility mechanism beyond mere justification.
evidence: 'Single atomic change (the constraints forbid a partial move).'
'no re-export shim (ADR Decision 4)'
No section of the plan names a revert strategy, a back-out commit, or a staging branch if post-merge failures surface.
findings.finding: The plan performs an irreversible atomic breaking rename (no re-export shim, no expand-contract) but provides no explicit back-out / rollback mechanism. The T4 criterion requires a rollback plan or expand-contract sequencing as the remedy, not just justification of the breaking choice. The plan's only recovery path is implied ('it's a single commit') but never stated.
findings.location: Approach section; Scope ('no shims'); AC list (no rollback AC)
scenarios: A grep miss leaves one patch string at the old path; the suite breaks in CI after merge; the team has no documented procedure for reverting beyond 'git revert the commit'.
An integration environment loads the moved modules differently (e.g. installed editable wheel caches the old paths); rollback steps are undefined.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' paragraph to the Approach section: 'If post-merge breakage is discovered, revert the single commit (`git revert <sha>`); because no shim exists the revert is clean. Re-run `make test` against the reverted tree to confirm the old paths are restored. Re-attempt the move only after identifying the missed reference.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
